actions.description: If 15% of commuters carpool in the next 2 hours, AQI can drop by ~6%
actions.title: Promote Carpooling

actions.description: Switching to public transport reduces vehicle emissions by 60-70%
actions.title: Use Public Transport

actions.description: For AQI above 150, wear N95 or FFP2 masks when outdoors. Masks reduce PM2.5 exposure by 80-95%. Essential for children, elderly, and those with respiratory conditions.
actions.title: Wear N95/FFP2 Masks

actions.description: Close windows, use air purifiers with HEPA filters, avoid smoking/cooking that generates smoke. Keep indoor AQI below 50 for safe breathing.
actions.title: Improve Indoor Air Quality

actions.description: High AQI increases breathing rate during exercise, exposing you to 5-10x more pollutants. Exercise indoors or postpone outdoor activities.
actions.title: Avoid Outdoor Exercise

actions.description: Children, elderly, pregnant women, and those with heart/lung conditions should stay indoors. AQI above 200 is very unhealthy for all.
actions.title: Vulnerable Groups: Stay Indoors

actions.description: Notify CPCB (Central Pollution Control Board) and local environmental agencies about high pollution levels for immediate action
actions.title: Alert Local Authorities

actions.description: Stay hydrated, eat antioxidant-rich foods (berries, green tea), use saline nasal sprays, and monitor symptoms like coughing or eye irritation.
actions.title: General Health Precautions

alerts.message: Current AQI is 201 - Unhealthy conditions detected
